meta_title: For Founders: email automation to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Solve Project Stagnation with Email Automation
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to implement automated email workflows using Antigravity and Claude Code to maintain momentum and reach your MVP faster.
content.answer_block: You aren't failing because you lack ideas; you're failing because of manual friction. By automating your user onboarding and feedback loops via email, you offload the 'busy work' that kills momentum. Use Antigravity to structure your logic and Claude Code to deploy robust, revenue-generating automated funnels in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'almost-finished' projects. You start with high-octane energy, but as the complexity of user retention and feedback loops grows, your momentum hits a wall. The silent killer of every founder's dream isn't a lack of code—it's the manual labor required to keep users engaged while you're still building.

Automation is your force multiplier. By integrating intelligent email flows directly into your MVP, you transform a stagnant project into a self-sustaining engine. This isn't just about sending emails; it's about building a system that validates your product and keeps your users hooked, even while you sleep.

steps.title: Define Your Trigger Logic
steps.description: Identify the high-value action in your app that warrants an automated follow-up. This creates a feedback loop that validates your product-market fit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Architect. Create a schema for a user-engagement trigger system. The system should track 'onboarding_complete' and 'feature_usage_milestone' events to trigger specific email sequences.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize trigger-logic.json schema for event-driven email notifications'

steps.title: Connect Your Email Service
steps.description: Integrate a transactional email provider like Resend or Postmark to ensure your automated emails actually hit the inbox, not the spam folder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust integration module for Resend API. Include error handling for failed deliveries and a queue system to prevent rate limiting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement resend-service.ts with robust error handling and queue management'

steps.title: Build the Sequence Engine
steps.description: Create a drip campaign that guides users through the core value proposition of your product. Focus on 'Aha!' moments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a state-machine based email scheduler. It should handle user onboarding sequences with specific delays between steps (Day 1, Day 3, Day 7).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create email-scheduler.ts to handle staggered drip campaign logic'

steps.title: Automate Feedback Loops
steps.description: Don't guess what your users want. Automate an email request for feedback after they reach a specific milestone in your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated feedback request module that triggers exactly 24 hours after a user successfully completes a core transaction.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement feedback-trigger.ts for post-transaction user outreach'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Use Claude Code to safely deploy your new automation service and monitor the logs for success metrics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that validates environment variables and runs smoke tests for the email service.

pro_tips: Never send generic 'Welcome' emails; segment your users by their first action taken in the app.
Use plain-text emails for higher deliverability and a more personal, 'founder-to-founder' feel.
Always include a 'Reply-to' address that goes directly to your personal inbox for high-touch feedback.
Implement a 'kill switch' for your automation in your database so you can pause flows during maintenance.
A/B test your subject lines early; a 10% increase in open rates is often the difference between churn and retention.
content.next_steps: Once your automated email flow is live, focus on analyzing the open rates. If users aren't engaging, iterate on the subject line or the timing of the trigger. When you hit a 30%+ open rate, move on to building your automated referral program.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum and start building for revenue. Our community of founders is waiting to help you ship your next iteration faster. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
